mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength, leading to higher expected corporate earnings and positive equity returns as the recovery solidifies.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC labor market recovery is a key focus for investors and the Fed.
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient (IC) is not statistically different from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, any acceleration in year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) will be interpreted as a signal that the Fed might taper asset purchases or hike rates sooner than expected, causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Market is sensitive to inflation surprises during the ZIRP era, following the 2013 'Taper Tantrum'.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in over 50% of instances where the inflation acceleration trigger is met during a ZIRP regime.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a self-sustaining consumer-led expansion, which drives corporate revenues and boosts equity prices. The signal is only active when both trends are present.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: A model using the interaction term does not show a statistically significant improvement in Sharpe ratio over a model with only the individual series.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals a revival in manufacturing and business investment, leading to strong cyclical equity performance. This effect is strongest early in the business cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:Cyclical sectors often lead market recoveries.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power is not significantly higher in NBER-dated recovery periods compared to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points in 3 months signals the labor market is tightening quickly, raising expectations of wage inflation and a hawkish Fed response, which is negative for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed's dual mandate includes maximum employment, so rapid progress towards it can imply policy changes.
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger event, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator for broad economic activity, consumer spending on durables, and construction employment, creating a positive tailwind for the S&P 500 over a very short horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market recovery is a key component of the post-2008 economic narrative.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between HOUST 3-month changes and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not consistently positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Fed Funds rate is pinned at the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%), an increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) compresses real yields and signals the Fed is falling behind the curve, increasing equity risk premium and causing negative returns.
failure_condition: The negative relationship between CPIAUCSL changes and SPX returns does not hold or is weaker when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% compared to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating year-over-year growth in real GDP (GDPC1) is a direct measure of aggregate economic health, translating into higher aggregate corporate earnings and supporting higher equity valuations over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic output, though it is released with a lag and subject to revision.
failure_condition: The correlation between YoY GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive over the full sample.
